label: 2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 3

extraction.one_line_summary: BENNETT Sam won his second consecutive sprint in Breda, launched by VAN POPPEL Danny inside 750m after the breakaway was caught at 11.4km to go.
tactical_patterns: ACKERMANN Pascal's leadout went too early before final turn, allowing BENNETT to come from behind with better timed launch.
Trek-Segafredo controlled all day but lacked final execution, exposing workload-to-results mismatch in flat sprint stages.
VAN POPPEL Danny's repeated perfect delivery suggests Bora sprint train peaks later than rivals, exploiting early launcher fatigue.
CARAPAZ Richard chain mechanical 11.4km out required RODRÍGUEZ Carlos and SIVAKOV Pavel relay, revealing Ineos GC protection hierarchy.

rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: positive: two consecutive wins with different positioning suggests adaptability beyond pure speed, can win from chaos or organization
rider_signals.VAN POPPEL Danny: positive: consecutive stage leadouts demonstrate exceptional timing discipline, understands when to commit final man better than rival trains
rider_signals.MERLIER Tim: negative: out of position on flat sprint suggests positioning weakness or team support gap compared to Bora/Trek setups
rider_signals.PEDERSEN Mads: neutral: second place again shows speed but inability to beat BENNETT when both positioned, may lack final acceleration differential
rider_signals.MCLAY Daniel: positive: third place from early dig indicates confidence to sprint from distance, alternative tactic when boxed becomes viable
rider_signals.DE GENDT Thomas: neutral: took KOM points after team loss of CRAS Steff, team morale management role emerging beyond breakaway specialist
rider_signals.WOODS Michael: negative: tangled with EINHORN Itamar and concussion exit, suggests positioning fragility or concentration lapse on flat terrain
